hero.title: 4 Days in North Vietnam.
hero.intro: Two full days in Hanoi — Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum, the Temple of Literature, Ba Dinh Square — followed by a luxury overnight cruise through Halong Bay's UNESCO World Heritage karst landscape. More time. More depth. The same iconic North Vietnam, at a pace that lets it land properly.
hero.heroImageAlt: Halong Bay luxury cruise boat limestone karsts sunset Vietnam

includes: All tours and sightseeing per itinerary
excludes: Vietnam e-visa (not required for Malaysians under 30 days)

overview.heading: 4 Days North Vietnam: Hanoi Fully Explored
body: This four-day package adds a proper Hanoi city day to the classic Halong Bay overnight cruise — giving you two full days in the Vietnamese capital before the bay. For travellers who want to understand Hanoi rather than just transit through it, those extra hours make a significant difference.

The Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum complex anchors day two: the embalmed body of Ho Chi Minh in its marble hall, the One Pillar Pagoda beside it, the presidential palace and gardens, and the Ho Chi Minh Museum — a sequence of sites that explains modern Vietnam's founding more clearly than any book. The Temple of Literature, founded in 1070 and dedicated to Confucius, is Vietnam's oldest university and one of its most beautiful heritage sites: five walled courtyards, stone doctoral steles, and a central well reflection pool.

Halong Bay on days three and four provides the counterpoint: immense natural space after two days of dense urban culture. The luxury cruise format means a smaller vessel, better food, and more attentive crew — the kind of overnight that feels genuinely restful rather than simply a floating transfer.

highlights.heading: Why This 4-Day North Vietnam Package Stands Apart

items.title: Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum Complex
items.body: The mausoleum where Ho Chi Minh has lain since 1975 is Vietnam's most visited and most politically significant site. The marble-and-granite structure on Ba Dinh Square is where he declared independence in 1945 and where his body now rests in a refrigerated glass casket. Adjacent are the One Pillar Pagoda — a lotus-shaped Buddhist temple on a single pillar above a pond, built in 1049 — and the Presidential Palace, a colonial governor's residence where Ho Chi Minh refused to live, preferring a stilt house in the garden. The complex is a complete education in Vietnamese modern history.
items.imageAlt: Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum Ba Dinh Square Hanoi Vietnam

items.title: Temple of Literature — Vietnam's Oldest University
items.body: Founded in 1070 under Emperor Ly Thanh Tong, the Temple of Literature (Van Mieu) served as Vietnam's National Academy for over 700 years, educating the country's mandarin class in Confucian scholarship. Five walled courtyards open one into the next, each with its own character: gateways, gardens, ceremonial wells, and 82 stone doctoral steles mounted on stone turtles — the names of doctoral graduates from 1442 to 1779 still legible on the stone. The Great House of Ceremonies at the far end is the most intact Imperial-era structure in Hanoi.
items.imageAlt: Temple of Literature Van Mieu Hanoi Vietnam ancient courtyard

items.title: Halong Bay Luxury Cruise
items.body: The overnight cruise format is the definitive way to experience Halong Bay — a limestone karst landscape that extends for 1,553 square kilometres and contains over 2,000 islands, most uninhabited, most unnamed. On a smaller luxury vessel, the experience is quieter and more personal than a large cruise ship. Kayaking lets you enter sea caves and lagoons inaccessible from the main boat. The squid fishing at night, off the back deck, is a ritual every crew member takes seriously. Sung Sot Cave — the Surprising Cave, its vaulted chamber 10 storeys high — remains the single most spectacular geological formation in the bay.
items.imageAlt: Halong Bay luxury cruise overnight boat karsts Vietnam

items.title: Hanoi Old Quarter Night Walk
items.body: After dark, the Old Quarter becomes its most intense version: mobile kitchens materialise on pavements, ba hoi (corner beer) stalls fill with office workers, and the street food activity reaches its peak around 7–9pm. Bun cha — grilled pork patties with vermicelli and a sweet-and-sour broth — was the dish Anthony Bourdain ordered when he ate here with Barack Obama in 2016. Banh mi, pho ga, and bun bo Nam Bo are all within walking distance. This evening food culture is one of the reasons Hanoi is consistently ranked among Asia's best cities for street food.
items.imageAlt: Hanoi Old Quarter night street food Vietnam evening

days.title: Arrive Hanoi — Old Quarter & Hoan Kiem Lake
days.description: Arrive at Noi Bai International Airport, met by your Dhesu guide and transferred to central Hanoi. Afternoon walk through the Old Quarter — the 36 guild streets, Hoan Kiem Lake, and Ngoc Son Temple on its island, reached by the red lacquer The Huc Bridge. The late afternoon light on the lake is the best time to visit. Evening free in the Old Quarter for street food.
suggestedActivities: Noi Bai Airport arrival and hotel transfer
Hoan Kiem Lake and Ngoc Son Temple

days.title: Hanoi City Tour — Mausoleum, Temple of Literature & West Lake
days.description: Full day Hanoi city tour. Morning: Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum complex (Ba Dinh Square, One Pillar Pagoda, Presidential Palace and stilt house, Ho Chi Minh Museum). The mausoleum itself requires modest dress and closed-toe shoes; photography inside the mausoleum hall is not permitted. Mid-morning: Temple of Literature (Van Mieu) — five courtyards, 82 doctoral steles, and the best-preserved example of traditional Vietnamese architecture in the capital. Afternoon: West Lake (Ho Tay) and Tran Quoc Pagoda, the oldest Buddhist pagoda in Hanoi, sitting on a small peninsula in the lake. Evening: Hoa Lo Prison Museum ('Hanoi Hilton') for a sobering account of both French colonial and Vietnam War history.
suggestedActivities: Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum and Ba Dinh Square
Presidential Palace gardens and stilt house
West Lake and Tran Quoc Pagoda

days.title: Transfer to Halong Bay — Luxury Overnight Cruise
days.meals: Breakfast, Lunch on cruise, Dinner on cruise
days.description: Morning transfer to Halong Bay (approximately 3.5 hours by private vehicle). Board your luxury overnight cruise vessel around noon. The boat moves out into the bay as you eat lunch on deck with the karst landscape unfolding around you. Afternoon: visit Sung Sot Cave (Surprising Cave) — the largest cave in Halong Bay, its vaulted chambers reaching 10 storeys high. Optional kayaking through smaller lagoons and sea caves. Sunset from the deck as the karsts turn amber. Squid fishing off the back of the boat after dark. Dinner on board; overnight on the bay.
suggestedActivities: Hanoi to Halong Bay transfer (3.5 hrs)

days.title: Halong Bay Morning — Return Hanoi — Depart
days.meals: Breakfast on cruise, Brunch on cruise
days.description: Early morning on the bay — tai chi on deck is offered by most cruise crews at sunrise. The bay at dawn, when mist rises between the karsts and the water goes glassy, is Halong Bay at its most photogenic. Brunch on board as the cruise returns to the harbour. Disembark and return to Hanoi (3.5 hours). Transfer to Noi Bai International Airport for departure.
suggestedActivities: Dawn on Halong Bay (photography / tai chi)

whyBook.heading: Vietnam Arranged by the Agency Malaysians Have Trusted Since 1988
whyBook.body: Dhesu has been arranging Vietnam journeys for Malaysian travellers since 1988. On Halong Bay specifically, we know which cruise operators maintain their vessels properly, which guides understand the geology and history of the karst landscape, and how to time the bay visit for the best conditions. Our guides in Hanoi know the city's history — colonial, revolutionary, contemporary — and can give context to the mausoleum, the Old Quarter, and everything in between.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. Vietnam is one of our most booked destinations — we have arranged hundreds of trips and know every aspect of the logistics.

points.title: 35+ Years of Vietnam Experience
points.body: Since 1988, we have been sending Malaysian travellers to Vietnam. Our guides and cruise partners are long-term trusted relationships, not anonymous contractors.

points.title: Flexible Departures
points.body: This package runs on your dates. No group minimums, no waiting. Your guide and Halong Bay cruise are booked for your specific dates.

points.title: Transparent Pricing
points.body: The price shown includes hotel, cruise, guide, vehicle, and transfers. No port fees, cruise surcharges, or hidden additions.

sections.heading: 4 Day Hanoi and Halong Bay Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
A 4-day Hanoi and Halong Bay tour from Malaysia adds a proper city day to the classic overnight bay cruise — making it the most popular North Vietnam package for Malaysian travellers who want both the capital and the UNESCO landscape. Two full days in Hanoi is enough to see the Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum complex, the Temple of Literature, and the Old Quarter without rushing.

Halong Bay's UNESCO listing reflects both its scenic and geological significance: the karst landscape was formed over 500 million years through tectonic movement and ocean erosion, creating a sea field of 2,000 limestone towers that rise up to 200 metres above the water. The overnight cruise format lets you see the bay at different hours — afternoon arrival, sunset, dawn — which is the only way to properly appreciate the scale and atmosphere.

sections.heading: Hanoi City Tour: What to See in Two Days
sections.body:
Hanoi rewards two full days better than almost any capital in Southeast Asia. The Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum complex (mausoleum, One Pillar Pagoda, Presidential Palace, Ho Chi Minh Museum) takes a full morning and requires modest dress. The Temple of Literature takes another hour. West Lake and its ancient pagodas fill the afternoon. The Old Quarter is best explored on foot, ideally in the morning or evening when the light is softer and the street food is at its best.

What most visitors don't expect is how much of Hanoi's history is still physically present: the French colonial boulevards, the imperial-era citadel, the ancient guild streets, and the revolutionary museums are all within walking or cycling distance of each other. Your guide provides the context that turns a list of sites into a coherent story.

faq.heading: Questions About This 4-Day Hanoi & Halong Bay Package

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for Vietnam?
items.answer: No. Malaysian passport holders can enter Vietnam visa-free for stays up to 30 days. No advance application is required — simply arrive at Noi Bai International Airport with your Malaysian passport and pass through immigration. No fee, no paperwork before departure.

items.question: What is the difference between the 3-day and 4-day Halong Bay packages?
items.answer: The main difference is one additional full day in Hanoi. The 3-day package gives you an afternoon in Hanoi on arrival day, then transfers to Halong Bay the following morning. The 4-day package adds a complete Hanoi city tour day covering the Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum complex, Temple of Literature, and West Lake — a significant addition for anyone who wants to understand the Vietnamese capital beyond its street-food reputation.

items.question: What happens if the weather is bad on Halong Bay?
items.answer: Halong Bay operates year-round. In stormy or unsafe weather conditions, the cruise operator may modify the route or temporarily shelter in a protected bay — these decisions are made by the captain for passenger safety. The bay's limestone landscape provides significant weather protection, and the cruise itinerary (caves, kayaking, floating villages) is designed with weather flexibility in mind.

items.question: Can I visit the Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum?
items.answer: Yes, though the mausoleum has specific visiting requirements. It is closed on Mondays, Fridays, and for approximately two months each year (September to November) while Ho Chi Minh's body is maintained. Modest dress is required — shoulders and knees covered. Photography inside the mausoleum hall is not permitted. Your guide will confirm the schedule and dress code before your visit.

items.question: What meals are included?
items.answer: Breakfast is included daily at the hotel (Days 2 and 3 in Hanoi). On the Halong Bay cruise (Day 3 afternoon through Day 4 morning), all meals on the boat are included: welcome lunch, dinner, and breakfast/brunch before disembarkation. Day 1 (arrival day) dinner and Day 4 (after cruise return) lunch onwards are at your own expense.
